freeSearchText: ID: 10641  Path: PHO 0184 Untitled binder, June 21 1993 (10641)  Untitled binder June 21 1993 Untitled binder dedicated to the analysis of Ferrari brand identity. The material documents the historical transformation of the logos and official symbols of Maranello-based company. Of particular historical and artistic interest is dossier dedicated to bronze casting large (160 cm) model Prancing Horse. text illustrates production processes and is enriched by series high-quality color photographs depicting stages production sculpture. Specifically there are 23 fotocolor photographs prints on paper. June 21 1993 PHO 0184 PHO 0184 26 cm 31 cm cm Ferrari Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Photographic archive General Collection PHO 0184 Untitled binder June 21 1993 10641
detailsDescription: Untitled binder dedicated to the analysis of Ferrari's brand identity. The material documents the historical transformation of the logos and official symbols of the Maranello-based company. Of particular historical and artistic interest is the dossier dedicated to the bronze casting of a large (160 cm) model of the Prancing Horse. the text illustrates the production processes and is enriched by a series of high-quality color photographs depicting the stages of production of the sculpture. Specifically, there are: 23 fotocolor, 7 photographs, and 4 prints on paper.

Specifically collection consisting of approx. 20 CDs approx. 200 negatives and 30 black and white color photographs depicting racing cars F1 single-seaters approx. 150 slides fotocolor depicting Grand Prix races the world racing an envelope containing negatives fotocolor slides depicting the Ferrari 456 GT an envelope containing photographs color photos the interior exterior car The unit also includes loose material namely 20 CDs 250 depicting cars Ferrari events unidentified subjects 200 fotocolor Ferrari models exhibitions car components 20 postcards 456M GT 400 black white photographs various subjects (including cars interiors exteriors car interior components on track dealership workshop interiors bronze prancing horse statue). detailsDescription: Unit consisting of a collection of photographic documentation, negatives, color photos, slides, and CDs.  Specifically:   	a collection consisting of approx. 20 CDs, approx. 200 negatives and 30 black and white and color photographs depicting racing cars and F1 single-seaters, approx. 150 slides and 6 fotocolor depicting Grand Prix races and the world of racing; 	an envelope containing negatives, fotocolor, and slides depicting the Ferrari 456 GT; 	an envelope containing photographs, slides, color photos, and negatives of the interior and exterior of a car;   The unit also includes loose material, namely:  approx. 20 CDs; approx. 250 slides depicting cars, Ferrari events, and unidentified subjects; approx. 200 negatives and fotocolor depicting Ferrari models, exhibitions, and car components; 20 postcards of the Ferrari 456M GT; approx. 400 black and white and color photographs depicting various subjects (including: Ferrari cars - interiors and exteriors, car interior components, cars on the track, dealership and workshop interiors, bronze prancing horse statue).  There are also approximately 20 color photocopies of the Ferrari logo mark-up.

freeSearchText: ID: 10871  Path: CAT 0180 Ferrari Classiche, 2016 (10871)  Ferrari Classiche 2016 This publication presents the Ferrari Classiche project the department of excellence created to offer owners an exclusive service aimed at restoring older cars. The Classiche team is made up of personnel capable of tackling even the most complex restorations.  Among services offered by Ferrari Certification Authenticity is particularly noteworthy. Only Maranello can perform this certification which is reserved for road-going Ferraris over 20 years old and for F1 Sport and Sport-Prototype cars with no age restrictions. The criteria for authenticity are also indicated namely vehicle conformity with original design and its full functionality. Another service offered maintenance classic cars which spare parts are used. This service does not only cover common spare parts but can also produce castings identical to originals supply structural components.  There are original copies. 2016 CAT 0180 CAT 0180 44 Italiano Inglese Cartaceo 30 cm 19 cm cm Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Catalogs leaflets price lists 3092 2000s 3846 CAT 0180 2016 10871
detailsDescription: This publication presents the Ferrari Classiche project, the department of excellence created to offer owners an exclusive service aimed at restoring older cars. The Classiche team is made up of personnel capable of tackling even the most complex restorations.   Among the services offered by Ferrari Classiche, the Certification of Authenticity is particularly noteworthy. Only Maranello can perform this certification, which is reserved for road-going Ferraris over 20 years old and for Ferrari F1, Sport and Sport-Prototype cars with no age restrictions. The criteria for authenticity are also indicated, namely the vehicle's conformity with the original design and its full functionality.  Another service offered is the maintenance of classic cars, for which Ferrari spare parts are used. This service does not only cover common spare parts, but Ferrari can also produce castings identical to the originals and supply structural components.   There are 7 original copies.

freeSearchText: ID: 10891  Path: CAT 0182 La Ferrari 1997 Annual Review, 1997 (10891)  La Ferrari 1997 Annual Review 1997 This publication presents the year 1997 for the Ferrari car manufacturer. It is divided into chapters the first part is dedicated to the history of car manufacturer and its current structure  with in-depth information on Ferrari markets Carrozzeria Scaglietti programme Wind Tunnel Fiorano track Driver Training courses races in which participates and its managers. freeSearchText: ID: 10918  Path: CAT 0186 Ferrari Formula One Championship 2015 Media Book, 2015 (10918)  Ferrari Formula One Championship 2015 Media Book 2015 For the year 2015 Scuderia Ferrari decided to publish series of small books each dedicated to one of the Formula One Grand Prix races of that year.  Each book covers the circuit schedule practice sessions qualifying and race illustrates SF15-T (chassis integration brakes transmission weight and tires engine ERS system power unit) presents team drivers sponsors and official suppliers analyzes Ferrari performance in Grand Prix over years  the race participants drivers constructors standings race report summary races preceding one to which book is dedicated. This is list books dedicated GPs published in GP Australia Melbourne 15/03/2015 there are 2 original copies. GP Malaysia - Kuala Lumpur 29/03/2015 there are original 2 copies. detailsTitle: Ferrari Formula One Championship 2015 Media Book, 2015
detailsDescription: For the year 2015, Scuderia Ferrari decided to publish a series of small books, each dedicated to one of the Formula One Grand Prix races of that year.   Each book covers the circuit, the schedule of practice sessions, qualifying and the race, illustrates the SF15-T (chassis, integration, brakes, transmission, weight and tires, engine, ERS system, power unit), presents the team, drivers, sponsors, and official suppliers, analyzes Ferrari's performance in the Grand Prix over the years,  the race participants, the drivers' and constructors' standings, the race report, and a summary of the races preceding the one to which the book is dedicated.  This is the list of books dedicated to GPs published in 2015:   	GP Australia - Melbourne 15/03/2015: there are 2 original copies. 	GP Malaysia - Kuala Lumpur 29/03/2015: there are original 2 copies. 	GP China - Shanghai 12/04/2015: there isn't any original copie. 	GP Bahrain - Sakhir 19/04/2015: there isn't any original copie. freeSearchText: ID: 10935  Path: Giuliano Michelotto, December 15, 1984 (10935)  Giuliano Michelotto December 15 1984 Typewritten letter from Giuliano Michelotto of Officina Michelotto in Padua informing Enzo Ferrari of the firm sports activities during 1984.  They list the victories achieved and participation in rallies mention the 308 IMSA in whose project Ferrari is involved.  Hoping to use GTO evolved version so as not to give competitors further technical advantages they will continue to use 308s racing programs.  The handwritten notes violet ink by Enzo Ferrari useful for reply state “received 27-12-84 GTO evolution. will talk him about it when he comes with pleasure.” December 15 1984 15-12-1984 15-12-1984 Letter Other Notes on letter 620 Italiano Cartaceo Folder Corrispondenza Enzo 1988 Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Correspondence 5891 Private letters 5892 1988 10939 Giuliano December 15 1984 10935

https://f1947.archivio.com/SYSTEM - Archivio Storico Pubblico Ferrari (Ente Comunicazione)/THRON DIGITAL ARCHIVE/SPORTS CARS/1984_GTO/1988 CORR/CORR_X_1988_010.jpeg

detailsDescription: Typewritten letter from Giuliano Michelotto of Officina Michelotto in Padua informing Enzo Ferrari of the firm's sports activities during 1984.  They list the victories achieved and participation in rallies, mention the 308 IMSA in whose project Ferrari is involved.  Hoping to use the GTO in the evolved version so as not to give competitors further technical advantages, they will continue to use the 308s in racing programs.   The handwritten notes in violet ink by Enzo Ferrari useful for the reply state, “received 27-12-84 x GTO evolution. i will talk to him about it when he comes with pleasure.”

preferredLabel: Angela Vettese, Rotella e il cinema, Skira editore, 2002
freeSearchText: ID: 10944  Path: PUB 0524 Angela Vettese, Rotella e il cinema, Skira editore, 2002 (10944)  Angela Vettese Rotella il cinema Skira editore 2002 The volume explores the symbiotic relationship between Mimmo Rotella and cinematic imagery. In the opening essay Quando brillavano le stelle (When the Stars Shone) Angela Vettese analyses how artist elevated film poster to an archaeological artefact of modernity transforming faces of stars (from Marilyn to Clark Gable) into timeless icons through gesture of “tearing”. The central section Tavole apparati (Illustrations) offers rich iconographic selection décollages created between 1960s and 1990s documenting Rotella technical and poetic evolution. Finally synoptic chronology edited by Katia Anguelova contextualises artist life within history cinema major events 20th century making book an essential tool for understanding birth pop culture in Italy. No. 10 original copies are available.  2002 Angela Vettese Skira editore PUB 0524 PUB 0524 137 Italiano Cartaceo 25 cm 29 cm cm Ferrari Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Publications 1010 PUB 0524 il cinema Skira editore 2002 10944
detailsTitle: Angela Vettese, Rotella e il cinema, Skira editore, 2002
detailsDescription: The volume explores the symbiotic relationship between Mimmo Rotella and cinematic imagery.  In the opening essay, Quando brillavano le stelle (When the Stars Shone), Angela Vettese analyses how the artist elevated the film poster to an archaeological artefact of modernity, transforming the faces of stars (from Marilyn to Clark Gable) into timeless icons through the gesture of “tearing”.  The central section, Tavole apparati (Illustrations), offers a rich iconographic selection of décollages created between the 1960s and 1990s, documenting Rotella's technical and poetic evolution.  Finally, the synoptic chronology edited by Katia Anguelova contextualises the artist's life within the history of cinema and the major events of the 20th century, making the book an essential tool for understanding the birth of pop culture in Italy.     No. 10 original copies are available.

freeSearchText: ID: 10957  Path: PUB 0525 L. Acerbi, L. Greggio, 1947 - 2007. Ferrari, Giorgio Nada Editore, 2007 (10957)  L. Acerbi L. Greggio 1947 2007. Ferrari Giorgio Nada Editore 2007 This monumental work documents year by year the entire history of Ferrari from Enzo Ferrari initial ideas to the successes of the modern era  published on occasion of company 60th anniversary. The book is structured strictly chronologically sixty-one chapters one for each year from 1947 to 2007 guide reader through evolution brand. Each chapter opens with general text that contextualises recounting company challenges and sporting victories. The heart each section is dedicated to presentation cars treating both racing cars (Formula Sport Prototypes and Gran Turismo) and road cars with equal dignity. The iconography is impressive alternating rare historical archive photos with colour images allowing readers follow development Cavallino design mechanics. final part volume an indispensable resource for collectors historians. appendices contain wealth technical competitive data. 2007 L. Acerbi Greggio Giorgio Nada Editore PUB 0525 PUB 0525 405 Italiano Cartaceo 24 cm 27 cm cm Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Publications 1010 PUB 0525 Acerbi Greggio 1947 Giorgio Nada Editore 10957
detailsTitle: L. Acerbi, L. Greggio, 1947 - 2007. Ferrari, Giorgio Nada Editore, 2007
detailsDescription: This monumental work documents, year by year, the entire history of Ferrari, from Enzo Ferrari's initial ideas to the successes of the modern era, published on the occasion of the company's 60th anniversary.  The book is structured strictly chronologically: sixty-one chapters, one for each year from 1947 to 2007, guide the reader through the evolution of the brand. Each chapter opens with a general text that contextualises the year, recounting the company's challenges and sporting victories. The heart of each section is dedicated to the presentation of the cars, treating both racing cars (Formula 1, Sport Prototypes and Gran Turismo) and road cars with equal dignity. The iconography is impressive, alternating rare historical archive photos with colour images, allowing readers to follow the development of the Cavallino's design and mechanics.  The final part of the volume is an indispensable resource for collectors and historians. The appendices contain a wealth of technical and competitive data.

Through contributions from Ciattini Cappelli and Capucci work retraces memory of archived road races and describes radical technical transformation of facility into centre of excellence for Formula testing. The book combines chronicle meeting organised by Ferrari Club Italia with an analytical description structural interventions that made track “princely racetrack”. VV. AA. detailsTitle: VV. AA., Ferrari Mugello, Giorgio Nada Editore, 1991
detailsDescription: Published by Giorgio Nada Editore in 1991, this volume celebrates the bond between Ferrari and the Mugello Circuit, documenting the historic gathering on 21-22 September 1991 that involved over two hundred Prancing Horse cars. Through contributions from Ciattini, Cappelli and Capucci, the work retraces the memory of archived road races and describes the radical technical transformation of the facility into a centre of excellence for Formula 1 testing. The book combines the chronicle of the meeting organised by the Ferrari Club Italia with an analytical description of the structural interventions that made the track a “princely racetrack”.

The sporting narrative is central and is divided between technical analysis of Formula single-seaters story exploits great champions and tribute to “other queens racing” namely Sports Prototype cars that triumphed in endurance races. There is also focus on road cars defined as “jewels production” which have transformed brand into global status symbol. The collection closes with technical section figures characteristics offering valuable archive data performance competitive results. detailsTitle: VV. AA., I cinquant'anni della Ferrari, La Stampa, 2007
detailsDescription: The volume opens with key figures Enzo Ferrari, the founder, and Luca di Montezemolo, the man behind the rebirth, analysing in parallel the evolution of the factory as an example of industrial excellence. The sporting narrative is central and is divided between the technical analysis of Formula 1 single-seaters, the story of the exploits of the great champions and the tribute to the “other queens of racing”, namely the Sports and Prototype cars that triumphed in endurance races. There is also a focus on road cars, defined as “jewels of production”, which have transformed the brand into a global status symbol. The collection closes with a technical section of figures and characteristics, offering a valuable archive of data, performance and competitive results. In short, the book is a dynamic and comprehensive guide, combining the rigour of statistical data with the epic charm of the history of the Prancing Horse.

Auction catalogue RM Auctions Sotheby 2017 This prestigious catalogue produced by RM Auctions in collaboration with Sotheby documents the celebratory auction ‘Ferrari Leggenda Passione’ held in Maranello on September 2017 on the occasion of the celebrations for 70th anniversary of Prancing Horse. The work presented in an elegant ring binder format is structured three main sections that guide collector through general information about event analysis of lots and legal conditions sale. The main body catalogue examines lots 102 to 154 which include both rare memorabilia and historic and modern cars exceptional collectible value. The main publication is enriched by small summary brochure dedicated exclusively to 288 GTO highlighting iconic importance this model within auction. In short volume is not only technical tool for auction but also valuable archival document that bears witness to one most significant moments Maranello recent history celebrating legend Ferrari its beating heart. No. original copies are available. 2017 VV. AA. detailsTitle: VV. AA., Auction catalogue, RM Auctions - Sotheby's, 2017
detailsDescription: This prestigious catalogue, produced by RM Auctions in collaboration with Sotheby's, documents the celebratory auction ‘Ferrari Leggenda e Passione’ held in Maranello on 9 September 2017, on the occasion of the celebrations for the 70th anniversary of the Prancing Horse. The work, presented in an elegant ring binder format, is structured in three main sections that guide the collector through general information about the event, analysis of the lots and the legal conditions of sale. The main body of the catalogue examines lots 102 to 154, which include both rare memorabilia and historic and modern cars of exceptional collectible value. The main publication is enriched by a small summary catalogue and a brochure dedicated exclusively to the 288 GTO, highlighting the iconic importance of this model within the auction. In short, the volume is not only a technical tool for the auction, but also a valuable archival document that bears witness to one of the most significant moments in Maranello's recent history, celebrating the legend of Ferrari in its beating heart.  No. 5 original copies are available.

preferredLabel: G. Celant, F. Trevisani, (a cura di), Maurizio Mochetti, Skira editore, 2003
freeSearchText: ID: 10965  Path: PUB 0530 G. Celant, F. Trevisani, (a cura di), Maurizio Mochetti, Skira editore, 2003 (10965)  G. Celant F. Trevisani (a cura di) Maurizio Mochetti Skira editore 2003 Published by Skira in 2003 this volume is the catalogue of the retrospective exhibition dedicated to Maurizio Mochetti held at the Palazzo Ducale in Sassuolo between September and November of same year. Curated by two leading figures in art criticism Germano Celant and Filippo Trevisani work analyses technological and conceptual rigour of an artist who made light space aerodynamics his main tools. The critical journey begins with an essay by Germano Celant entitled “Maurizio Mochetti arrow archer target” which explores dynamic tension geometric precision underlying each artist projects seen as an act aiming towards aesthetic physical goal. This is followed contribution Filippo Trevisani who introduces philosophical concept “Kairos” (the opportune moment) interpreting work as perfect realisation intuition time space. The main body book documents artist vast output through section “Works 1964 2003” allowing us to retrace forty years research from his early experiments with lasers to his more complex minimalist installations. The volume is completed rigorous documentary section that includes biographical chronology exhaustive bibliography making it essential tool for study one most original protagonists Italian contemporary art. No. original copies are available. 2003 G. F. (a cura di) Skira editore PUB 0530 PUB 0530 251 Italiano Cartaceo 24 cm 28 cm cm Ferrari Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Publications 1010 PUB 0530 cura di Maurizio editore 10965
detailsTitle: G. Celant, F. Trevisani, (a cura di), Maurizio Mochetti, Skira editore, 2003
detailsDescription: Published by Skira in 2003, this volume is the catalogue of the retrospective exhibition dedicated to Maurizio Mochetti held at the Palazzo Ducale in Sassuolo between September and November of the same year. Curated by two leading figures in art criticism, Germano Celant and Filippo Trevisani, the work analyses the technological and conceptual rigour of an artist who made light, space and aerodynamics his main tools.  The critical journey begins with an essay by Germano Celant entitled “Maurizio Mochetti: arrow, archer and target”, which explores the dynamic tension and geometric precision underlying each of the artist's projects, seen as an act of aiming towards an aesthetic and physical goal. This is followed by a contribution by Filippo Trevisani, who introduces the philosophical concept of “Kairos” (the opportune moment), interpreting Mochetti's work as the perfect realisation of an intuition in time and space.  The main body of the book documents the artist's vast output through the section “Works 1964 - 2003”, allowing us to retrace forty years of research, from his early experiments with lasers to his more complex and minimalist installations. The volume is completed by a rigorous documentary section that includes a biographical chronology and an exhaustive bibliography, making it an essential tool for the study of one of the most original protagonists of Italian contemporary art.     No. 2 original copies are available.

freeSearchText: ID: 10970  Path: Peter S. Knoll, June 3, 1988 (10970)  Peter S. Knoll June 1988 Handwritten letter (in block capitals) sent by Mr. Peter S. Knoll from Bassano to Enzo Ferrari dated June 1988. The author describing himself as long-time and loyal customer who has purchased seven Ferrari cars over the last twenty-four years (the first at age eighteen) offers modest gift to the recipient. The core of the message is an explicit request for assistance in obtaining an F40. detailsDescription: Handwritten letter (in block capitals) sent by Mr. Peter S. Knoll from Bassano to Enzo Ferrari, dated June 3, 1988. The author, describing himself as a long-time and loyal customer who has purchased seven Ferrari cars over the last twenty-four years (the first at age eighteen), offers a "modest gift" to the recipient. The core of the message is an explicit request for assistance in obtaining an F40.    The document features a pencil annotation "R 7.6.88" at the bottom, indicating that the correspondence was processed or a reply was issued on June 7, 1988.

detailsDescription: Handwritten letter (in block capitals) in French sent by Michel Mas, a 16-year-old student from Zellenberg, France, to Enzo Ferrari, dated June 6, 1988. The text expresses the fan's profound admiration for the "super" quality of Ferrari cars, praising their sobriety, luxury, and finish. The author specifically highlights the F40 as the most prestigious car he has ever seen and thanks the "Ingegniere" for his immense contribution to the history of the automobile and motorsport. The original envelope, marked "Personnel" and postmarked June 28, 1988, is included.    No administrative annotations or signs of a reply are present on the document.

freeSearchText: ID: 10989  Path: PUB 0532 Giorgio Piola, Formula 1. Analisi tecnica 2002-2003, Giorgio Nada Editore, 2003 (10989)  Giorgio Piola Formula 1. Analisi tecnica 2002-2003 Giorgio Nada Editore 2003 This volume by Giorgio Piola dissects Formula engineering in the two-year period 2002-2003 drawing on an extensive collection of detailed technical illustrations documenting the evolution of engines aerodynamics and chassis. From an analysis of Ferrari supremacy to the departure Arrows team text explores mechanical solutions and racing strategies with visual rigor offering professional summary that is essential for understanding technological innovations and new regulations season. 2003 Piola Nada Editore PUB 0532 PUB 0532 115 Italiano Cartaceo 24 cm 27 cm cm Ferrari Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Publications 1010 PUB 0532 Formula Analisi tecnica 2002 2003 Nada Editore 10989
detailsTitle: Giorgio Piola, Formula 1. Analisi tecnica 2002-2003, Giorgio Nada Editore, 2003
detailsDescription: This volume by Giorgio Piola dissects Formula 1 engineering in the two-year period 2002-2003, drawing on an extensive collection of detailed technical illustrations documenting the evolution of engines, aerodynamics, and chassis. From an analysis of Ferrari's supremacy to the departure of the Arrows team, the text explores mechanical solutions and racing strategies with visual rigor, offering a professional summary that is essential for understanding the technological innovations and new regulations of the season.

detailsDescription: This volume by Giorgio Piola documents the technological peak of Formula 1 between 2004 and 2005, illustrating with extreme precision the evolution of engines, aerodynamics, and mechanical components. Through a rich gallery of technical drawings, the work analyzes winning strategies, regulatory changes, and the most sophisticated engineering solutions, including an unprecedented retrospective on iconic single-seaters of the past and a complete preview of the following season.

freeSearchText: ID: 10992  Path: Jean-Pierre Jarier, August 4, 1988 (10992)  Jean-Pierre Jarier August 1988 Fax sent by former French F1 and F2 driver Jean-Pierre Jarier wishing Enzo Ferrari all the best for quick recovery in good health.  He reveals that during his entire F1 career he never had the chance to become Ferrari driver and he regrets it.  To compensate for this lack he asks for information regarding the acquisition of an F40.  The relevant office responds that it has passed request on to Business Office recommending full reply.  August 1988 04-08-1988 05-08-1988 Jean-Pierre Jarier Resident Casabianca 17 Bd du Larelle 98000 Monaco Other Personal issue Administrative Office 573 Francese Italiano Cartaceo Folder Corrispondenza Enzo Ferrari 1988 Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Correspondence 5891 Private letters 5892 Jean Pierre August 10992

detailsDescription: Fax sent by former French F1 and F2 driver Jean-Pierre Jarier wishing Enzo Ferrari all the best for a quick recovery in good health.     He reveals that during his entire F1 career he never had the chance to become a Ferrari driver and he regrets it.  To compensate for this lack, he asks for information regarding the acquisition of an F40.     The relevant office responds that it has passed the request on to the Business Office, recommending a full reply.

freeSearchText: ID: 10997  Path: Claudio Leoz, July 3, 1988 (10997)  Claudio Leoz July 1988 Handwritten letter sent by Mr. Claudio G. Leon from Coronel Pringles (Argentina) to Enzo Ferrari dated July 1988. The text conveys the fan congratulations to Ferrari and drivers Michele Alboreto and Gerhard Berger for their 3rd and 4th place results at the French Grand Prix along with well wishes for the second half of season. The author requests photographs of team Formula cars as well as Sport models specifically mentioning F40 Testarossa regarding latter he notes seeing it in television series Miami Vice (Division Miami) with Don Johnson. He also mentions witnessing Ferraris driven by Ernesto Bessone in Buenos Aires. No administrative annotations or signs of reply are present on document. detailsDescription: Handwritten letter sent by Mr. Claudio G. Leon from Coronel Pringles (Argentina) to Enzo Ferrari, dated July 3, 1988. The text conveys the fan's congratulations to Ferrari and drivers Michele Alboreto and Gerhard Berger for their 3rd and 4th place results at the French Grand Prix, along with well wishes for the second half of the season. The author requests photographs of the team's Formula 1 cars as well as "Sport" models, specifically mentioning the F40 and the Testarossa; regarding the latter, he notes seeing it in the television series "Miami Vice" (Division Miami) with Don Johnson. He also mentions witnessing Ferraris driven by Ernesto Bessone in Buenos Aires.    No administrative annotations or signs of a reply are present on the document.

freeSearchText: ID: 11000  Path: Walter R. Paapst, May 24, 1988 (11000)  Walter R. Paapst May 24 1988 Handwritten letter from an 18-year-old Dutch boy sent to Enzo Ferrari on May 24 1988. The boy is very interested in Ferrari cars and joined Ferrari Club Nederland with membership no. 165. He reveals that he has every intention of becoming owner especially since he saw an F40 racing on Belgian circuit.  He would like to receive an autograph to add his collection of catalogues photos and brochures and encloses the photograph of the F40 mentioned above.  The handwritten note shows the return indications  giallo 7-6-88.  May 24 1988 24-05-1988 24-05-1988 Walter R. detailsDescription: Handwritten letter from an 18-year-old Dutch boy sent to Enzo Ferrari on May 24, 1988. The boy is very interested in Ferrari cars and joined Ferrari Club Nederland with membership no. 165. He reveals that he has every intention of becoming a Ferrari owner, especially since he saw an F40 racing on a Belgian circuit.  He would like to receive an autograph to add to his collection of Ferrari catalogues, photos, and brochures and encloses the photograph of the F40 mentioned above.   The handwritten note shows the return indications: giallo E 7-6-88.

freeSearchText: ID: 11001  Path: Harutoshi Komatsu, April 1, 1988 (11001)  Harutoshi Komatsu April 1988 Typed letter sent by Mr. Harutoshi Komatsu from Mishima City Japan to Enzo Ferrari containing the original air mail envelope. The author business owner and racing enthusiast explains that his company is named Dino in memory of Ferrari son. He recounts his history as Ferrari owner having purchased Dino in 1975 and 365 GT/4 BB in 1978 which he restored and intends to pass on to his own son. After mentioning victory the All Japan Championship he expresses devotion the brand. The Administrative Office replies on April 22 1988 stating that inquiry regarding purchase of F40 has been forwarded Sales Office for detailed response. detailsDescription: Typed letter sent by Mr. Harutoshi Komatsu from Mishima City, Japan, to Enzo Ferrari, containing the original air mail envelope. The author, a business owner and racing enthusiast, explains that his company is named "Dino" in memory of Ferrari's son. He recounts his history as a Ferrari owner, having purchased a Dino in 1975 and a 365 GT/4 BB in 1978, which he restored and intends to pass on to his own son. After mentioning his victory in the "All Japan Championship," he expresses his devotion to the brand.    The Administrative Office replies on April 22, 1988, stating that his inquiry regarding the purchase of a Ferrari F40 has been forwarded to the Sales Office for a detailed response.

Piloti the date 5.4.88 accompanied by stylized administrative mark indicating that request was processed by office on April 1988 likely with dispatch driver photographs. detailsDescription: Handwritten letter sent by Eirik R. Andersen, a 13-year-old boy from Norway, to Enzo Ferrari, dated March 25, 1988, containing several original drawings of Ferrari models (including the 348, Testarossa, GTO, F40, and 312 T4). The author expresses his deep interest in cars and his hobby of collecting autographs from the most influential figures in automotive history, noting that he already possesses those of Sergio Pininfarina, Giorgio Giugiaro, Ferry Porsche, and Lee Iacocca.    He requests a signed photograph of Enzo Ferrari to add to his collection. The document features a handwritten annotation in red ink "fot. Piloti" and the date "5.4.88" accompanied by a stylized administrative mark, indicating that the request was processed by the office on April 5, 1988, likely with the dispatch of driver photographs.

ID: 11005  Path: Adam Andrews, June 1, 1988 (11005)  Adam Andrews June 1988 Handwritten letter sent by Mr. Adam Andrews to Enzo Ferrari containing two black and white photographs. The text of the letter expresses the 17-year-old Australian fan interest in automotive design he shares pictures of his first attempt at designing Ferrari model constructed from cardboard. After describing technical details such as the air inlets and exhaust system he asks for Enzo Ferrari honest opinion on his work and requests detailed photographs of F40 model. The document features handwritten annotation F. 40 date 6.6.88 accompanied by stylized administrative initial indicating that correspondence was processed on June 1988. detailsDescription: Handwritten letter sent by Mr. Adam Andrews to Enzo Ferrari containing two black and white photographs. The text of the letter expresses the 17-year-old Australian fan's interest in automotive design: he shares pictures of his first attempt at designing a Ferrari, a model constructed from cardboard. After describing technical details such as the air inlets and exhaust system, he asks for Enzo Ferrari's honest opinion on his work and requests detailed photographs of the F40 model.    The document features a handwritten annotation "F. 40" and the date "6.6.88" accompanied by a stylized administrative initial, indicating that the correspondence was processed on June 6, 1988.

freeSearchText: ID: 11006  Path: Stanley Cohen, March 10, 1988 (11006)  Stanley Cohen March 10 1988 Typed letter from Stanley Cohen a U.S. collector to Enzo Ferrari. He encloses photograph of Testarossa-shaped fruit cake and he admits that he wanted to send it frozen via airmail but did not receive availability from the airline.  Therefore he will allocate it to technical college or the local Ferrari Club.  He writes that he has ordered an F40 from the dealer in Danbury Connecticut also owns other Ferrari models including GTO and asks if can meet Enzo Ferrari when picks up car in Maranello.  The copy of Enzo reply bears thank you for kind remembrance. detailsDescription: Typed letter from Stanley Cohen, a U.S. collector, to Enzo Ferrari. He encloses a photograph of a Testarossa-shaped fruit cake and he admits that he wanted to send it frozen via airmail, but did not receive availability from the airline.  Therefore, he will allocate it to a technical college or the local Ferrari Club.   He writes that he has ordered an F40 from the dealer in Danbury, Connecticut — he also owns other Ferrari models including a GTO, and asks if he can meet Enzo Ferrari when he picks up the car in Maranello.     The copy of Enzo Ferrari's reply bears a thank you for the kind remembrance.

freeSearchText: ID: 11007  Path: Ron Spangler, January 8, 1988 (11007)  Ron Spangler January 1988 Typed letter on Prancing Horse Farm letterhead sent by Mr. Ron Spangler to Enzo Ferrari Piero Lardi Ferrari and Niccola Materazzi dated January 1988. The author introduces the activities of the Prancing Horse Farm in Maryland highlighting his 25-year history as promoter of the brand and owner of 47 Ferrari cars. Mentioning his possession two race-winning chassis (#16407 and #38179) he expresses desire to purchase an F40 Competizione to race on international circuits. The General Manager G.B. Razelli replies on February 16 1988 (ref. 8/sb) informing Spangler that his request was passed by Enzo that precise answer can only be provided once F40 competition project has been officially started January 1988 08-01-1988 08-01-1988 Ron RLS Enterprices Inc. dba/ Prancing Horse Farm Ltd. detailsDescription: Typed letter on "Prancing Horse Farm" letterhead sent by Mr. Ron Spangler to Enzo Ferrari, Piero Lardi Ferrari, and Niccola Materazzi, dated January 8, 1988. The author introduces the activities of the "Prancing Horse Farm" in Maryland, highlighting his 25-year history as a promoter of the brand and owner of 47 Ferrari cars. Mentioning his possession of two race-winning chassis (#16407 and #38179), he expresses the desire to purchase an "F40 Competizione" to race on international circuits.    The General Manager, G.B. Razelli, replies on February 16, 1988 (ref. 8/sb), informing Spangler that his request was passed on by Enzo Ferrari and that a precise answer can only be provided once the F40 competition project has been officially started

Classic car auction 2006 2008 Yearbook Historica Selecta srl 2008 This volume by Adolfo Orsi and Raffaele Gazzi provides rigorous analytical documentation of international auctions held between August 2006 and July 31 2008 following the methodological approach of the twelve editions of Bolaffi catalog previously edited by authors. The work offers complete mapping vintage market including in chapter dedicated to seasonal results an exhaustive list every car produced and offered in most prestigious sales worldwide. This specific complimentary copy featuring Christmas card attached to first page is an essential professional tool for historical commercial evaluation automotive heritage in two-year period question. Adolfo Orsi Raffaele Gazzi Historica Selecta srl PUB 0539 PUB 0539 389 Inglese Cartaceo 24 cm 31 cm cm Ferrari Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Publications 1010 PUB 0539 Fuel for motoring lifestyle Hagerty Classic car auction 2006 Yearbook Historica Selecta srl 11017
detailsTitle: Adolfo Orsi, Raffaele Gazzi, Fuel for the motoring lifestyle. Hagerty's. Classic car auction 2006 - 2008 Yearbook, Historica Selecta srl, 2008
detailsDescription: This volume by Adolfo Orsi and Raffaele Gazzi provides a rigorous analytical documentation of international auctions held between August 1, 2006, and July 31, 2008, following the methodological approach of the twelve editions of the Bolaffi catalog previously edited by the authors. The work offers a complete mapping of the vintage market, including in the chapter dedicated to seasonal results an exhaustive list of every car produced and offered in the most prestigious sales worldwide. This specific complimentary copy, featuring a Christmas card attached to the first page, is an essential professional tool for the historical and commercial evaluation of automotive heritage in the two-year period in question.

preferredLabel: John Lamm, Ferrari. Stories from Those Who Lived the Legend, Motorbooks, 2007
freeSearchText: ID: 11018  Path: PUB 0540 John Lamm, Ferrari. Stories from Those Who Lived the Legend, Motorbooks, 2007 (11018)  John Lamm Ferrari. Stories from Those Who Lived the Legend Motorbooks 2007 This volume by John Lamm analyzes the evolution of Ferrari through chronological overview spanning decades from the 1940s to early 2000s. The text examines different facets of brand excellence focusing its analysis on key personalities who have marked each era from engineering successes to victories on track. detailsDescription: This professional biography by Luc Domenjoz traces Michael Schumacher's dazzling career, rigorously documenting his journey from his first steps in karting in 1973 to his consecration in Formula 1 in 1997. freeSearchText: ID: 11023  Path: PUB 0544 John Price Williams, Maserati. The road cars. 1981 - 1997, The Crowood Press, 2007 (11023)  John Price Williams Maserati. The road cars. 1981 1997 The Crowood Press 2007 This volume by John Price Williams published by The Crowood Press in 2007 takes rigorous historiographical look at Maserati production between 1981 and 1997 period marked by the debut of the Biturbo and the relaunch of Trident. Through careful technical and documentary analysis author traces evolution of iconic models such as Ghibli II Shamal Quattroporte IV illustrating transition from De Tomaso management to integration into Fiat Group. Enriched with production data sheets extensive photographic material text is an indispensable analytical resource for understanding Maserati technological stylistic transition towards modern engineering. 2007 John Price Williams Crowood Press PUB 0544 PUB 0544 176 Inglese Cartaceo 20 cm 25 cm cm Ferrari Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Publications 1010 PUB 0544 Maserati road cars 1981 1997 11023
detailsTitle: John Price Williams, Maserati. The road cars. 1981 - 1997, The Crowood Press, 2007
detailsDescription: This volume by John Price Williams, published by The Crowood Press in 2007, takes a rigorous historiographical look at Maserati's production between 1981 and 1997, a period marked by the debut of the Biturbo and the relaunch of the Trident. Through careful technical and documentary analysis, the author traces the evolution of iconic models such as the Ghibli II, the Shamal, and the Quattroporte IV, illustrating the transition from De Tomaso's management to integration into the Fiat Group. Enriched with production data sheets and extensive photographic material, the text is an indispensable analytical resource for understanding Maserati's technological and stylistic transition towards modern engineering.

Introduced by Roberto Loi and enriched by a preface by Lorenzo Ramaciotti, the volume features specialist texts by Giuliano Silli and rigorous iconographic research and documentation systematization by Angelo Tito Anselmi. freeSearchText: ID: 11025  Path: PUB 0546 Denis Asselberghs, 24 Heures-Uren Francorchamps, IHM, 1994 (11025)  Denis Asselberghs 24 Heures/Uren Francorchamps IHM 1994 This work edited by the prestigious journalist Denis Asselberghs and published by IHM is the definitive official yearbook of the 1994 edition of 24 Hours of Spa-Francorchamps. Written in French and Dutch volume offers detailed and technically rigorous account triumph BMW 318is driven by trio Ravaglia-Tassin-Burgstaller in context golden age Supertourisme cars. Thanks to high-quality illustrations meticulous analysis stages race book combines accurate statistical data with vibrant account one world most iconic motor racing marathons. The editorial quality wealth technical content make it an essential reference work for motorsport historians collectors capable recreating atmosphere an event that marked history endurance racing in Belgium. 1994 Denis Asselberghs IHM PUB 0546 PUB 0546 193 Francese Olandese Cartaceo 24 cm 32 cm cm Ferrari Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Publications 1010 PUB 0546 Heures Uren Francorchamps 11025
detailsDescription: This work, edited by the prestigious journalist Denis Asselberghs and published by IHM, is the definitive official yearbook of the 1994 edition of the 24 Hours of Spa-Francorchamps. Written in French and Dutch, the volume offers a detailed and technically rigorous account of the triumph of the BMW 318is, driven by the trio Ravaglia-Tassin-Burgstaller, in the context of the golden age of Supertourisme cars.  Thanks to high-quality illustrations and a meticulous analysis of the stages of the race, the book combines accurate statistical data with a vibrant account of one of the world's most iconic motor racing marathons. The editorial quality and wealth of technical content make it an essential reference work for motorsport historians and collectors, capable of recreating the atmosphere of an event that marked the history of endurance racing in Belgium.

detailsDescription: Typewritten letter from M. Gonot of Michelin Competitions Service to D. Marin employee of Charles Pozzi Ferrari France, specifying that he had studied the tire problem on the Ferrari F40/IMSA GTO.  Examination shows that the dimensions, which conform to the regulations and are compatible with the technical elements affecting the vehicle indicated, are not currently available.  Unfortunately, they are unable to supply the tires necessary for the development of the race car in the time frame indicated and remain available for a review of the problem.     Copy of the letter is forwarded to Enzo Ferrari.

Razelli regarding the Ferrari F40. The text dated May 18 1988 confirms the successful return journey to Paris and details 200 km test session on track in Savoy during Ferrari Club event. detailsDescription: Fax letter sent by Daniel Marin (Ferrari-France) to General Manager Ing. G.B. Razelli regarding the Ferrari F40. The text, dated May 18, 1988, confirms the successful return journey to Paris and details a 200 km test session on a track in Savoy during a Ferrari Club event.    Marin expresses great enthusiasm for the car’s exceptional engine power and road holding, describing it as a "true Ferrari" that meets all the requirements of sporting clients. He congratulates the entire team on the model's realisation, pointing out that customers are still unaware of the "monster" they have ordered. A list of minor points to be refined is mentioned as an attachment.    The document features a "RICEVUTO 19 MAG. 1988" stamp and the handwritten filing number "152".

preferredLabel: Ferrari Commercial Office to Mr. Brian David Kohn, February 4, 1988
freeSearchText: ID: 11031  Path: Ferrari Commercial Office to Mr. Brian David Kohn, February 4, 1988 (11031)  Ferrari Commercial Office to Mr. Brian David Kohn February 1988 Typed letter sent by the Commercial Office (ref. CO/CT/pf/041) to Mr. Brian David Kohn in London dated February 1988. The letter refers to previous communication from September 1987 and personal reply already sent by Enzo Ferrari regarding the purchase of Ferrari F40. The office explains that although the F40 was conceived as limited production model reserved for existing owners and this would be recipient first car of brand they will nonetheless try allocate vehicle him. For this reason Mr. Kohn has been officially placed on waiting list and letter concludes by stating that final answer will be provided within two three months. url.original: https://f1947.archivio.com/SYSTEM - Archivio Storico Pubblico Ferrari (Ente Comunicazione)/THRON DIGITAL ARCHIVE/SPORTS CARS/1987_F40/00_/04_CORR/1988 CORR/CORR_V_1988_001.jpeg

detailsTitle: Ferrari Commercial Office to Mr. Brian David Kohn, February 4, 1988
detailsDescription: Typed letter sent by the Commercial Office (ref. CO/CT/pf/041) to Mr. Brian David Kohn in London, dated February 4, 1988. The letter refers to a previous communication from September 1987 and a personal reply already sent by Enzo Ferrari regarding the purchase of a Ferrari F40. The office explains that although the F40 was conceived as a limited production model reserved for existing Ferrari owners and this would be the recipient's first car of the brand, they will nonetheless try to allocate a vehicle to him. For this reason, Mr. Kohn has been officially placed on the "waiting list," and the letter concludes by stating that a final answer will be provided within two to three months.

She requests prospectus of F40 and offers her congratulations for recent victories achieved by driver Gerhard Berger. The letter concludes with warm wishes for Christmas and New Year. No administrative annotations or signs of reply are present on document. detailsTitle: Elena and Enzo Dalla Gassa, January 25, 1988
detailsDescription: Handwritten letters from a couple of children thanking Enzo Ferrari for receiving special gifts, including a toy car they will keep in their window.  They hope to visit him as soon as their dad finishes the ceramic Ferrari he is modeling.   The parents of the two children in turn write a letter touched by Enzo Ferrari's generosity, who gives them toy cars including an F40.  They also reveal that they are finishing a ceramic model of the Testarossa, which their passion for the Prancing Horse makes it almost natural to do.

freeSearchText: ID: 11037  Path: Dogan Uezer, January 27, 1988 (11037)  Dogan Uezer January 27 1988 Handwritten letter in German sent by Mr. Dogan Uezer from Aarburg to Enzo Ferrari dated January 27 1988. The author dedicated fan who currently commutes to school by bicycle expresses his admiration for the brand and specifically for the latest model the Ferrari F40. The core of letter is humorous inquiry regarding meaning of name F40 for which he suggests several playful interpretations (e.g. related to 40-year-olds or speed of 40 km/h). The document features handwritten annotation at top summarizing request in Italian TIFOSO CHIEDE COSA SIGNIFICA 40 (Fan asks what 40 means). No administrative signs reply are present on sheet. [As Road Rat Magazine edition 15 Autumn 2023 reports name F40 was probably given due suggestion Italian motoring journalist and author Gino Rancati celebrate four decades Ferrari road car production.] January 27 1988 27-01-1988 27-01-1988 Dogan Uezer Schulhaus Paradiesli 4663 Aarburg Letter Other Missing 76 Folder Corrispondenza Enzo 1987 Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Correspondence 5891 Private letters 5892 1987 1988 11037

url.original: https://f1947.archivio.com/SYSTEM - Archivio Storico Pubblico Ferrari (Ente Comunicazione)/THRON DIGITAL ARCHIVE/SPORTS CARS/1987_F40/00_/04_CORR/1988 CORR/CORR_U_1988_017.jpeg

detailsDescription: Handwritten letter in German sent by Mr. Dogan Uezer from Aarburg to Enzo Ferrari, dated January 27, 1988. The author, a dedicated fan who currently commutes to school by bicycle, expresses his admiration for the brand and specifically for the latest model, the Ferrari F40. The core of the letter is a humorous inquiry regarding the meaning of the name "F40," for which he suggests several playful interpretations (e.g., related to 40-year-olds or a speed of 40 km/h).    The document features a handwritten annotation at the top summarizing the request in Italian: "TIFOSO: CHIEDE COSA SIGNIFICA F 40" (Fan: asks what F 40 means). No administrative signs of a reply are present on the sheet.  [As The Road Rat Magazine, edition 15, Autumn 2023 reports: the name of F40 was probably given due to the suggestion of Italian motoring journalist and author Gino Rancati to celebrate four decades of Ferrari road car production.]

freeSearchText: ID: 11038  Path: Carlo Massimo, November 2, 1987 (11038)  Carlo Massimo November 1987 Typed letter sent by Prof. Carlo Massimo to Enzo Ferrari dated November 1987. The author prominent customer who has purchased fourteen new Ferraris over fifteen years informs the Ingegnere of the total loss of his GTO which caught fire and was completely destroyed. He details long history of mechanical failures with the vehicle and describes frustration being unable to open engine hood to use an extinguisher due design locking mechanism. While praising technical assistance and hospitality shown by Director General Della Casa in Maranello he emphasizes that such grave incident should be brought Ferrari personal attention. The document features handwritten annotation in blue ink at top elementi di questo chiesto esaurientemente (information on this requested exhaustively). Enzo Ferrari personally replies on November 18 1987 expressing his sadness over loss GTO confirming commitment made by Della Casa provide Professor with new F40 under conditions particular regard. concludes looking forward meeting author when new car is delivered. 1987 02-11-1987 02-11-1987 Carlo Massimo via A. Mari 14 Fiesole Firenze Letter On road Copy EF message 677 Italiano Cartaceo Folder Corrispondenza Enzo Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Correspondence 5891 Private letters 5892 11038

detailsDescription: Typed letter sent by Prof. Carlo Massimo to Enzo Ferrari, dated November 2, 1987. The author, a prominent customer who has purchased fourteen new Ferraris over fifteen years, informs the "Ingegnere" of the total loss of his GTO, which caught fire and was completely destroyed. He details a long history of mechanical failures with the vehicle and describes the frustration of being unable to open the engine hood to use an extinguisher due to the design of the locking mechanism. While praising the technical assistance and the hospitality shown by Director General Della Casa in Maranello, he emphasizes that such a grave incident should be brought to Ferrari's personal attention.    The document features a handwritten annotation in blue ink at the top: "elementi di questo chiesto esaurientemente" (information on this requested exhaustively). Enzo Ferrari personally replies on November 18, 1987, expressing his sadness over the loss of the GTO and confirming a commitment made by Della Casa to provide the Professor with a new F40 under "conditions of particular regard." Ferrari concludes by looking forward to meeting the author when the new car is delivered.

preferredLabel: Ferrari General Management to Mr. Edward L.C. Smith, September 24, 1987
freeSearchText: ID: 11040  Path: Ferrari General Management to Mr. Edward L.C. Smith, September 24, 1987 (11040)  Ferrari General Management to Mr. Edward L.C. Smith September 24 1987 Typed letter sent by the Ferrari Commercial Office (ref. CO/EDC/pf/346) to Mr. Edward L.C. Smith in Santa Cruz California dated September 24 1987. The office informs the recipient that his previous letter of August 14 was forwarded to them by Enzo Ferrari. In response his interest in the new F40 model office clarifies that series of cars compliant with USA regulations will be produced and marketed through official sales network in his country. The letter concludes by suggesting that Mr. Smith contact nearest Ferrari dealer as soon as possible. No further handwritten annotations are present on document. detailsTitle: Ferrari General Management to Mr. Edward L.C. Smith, September 24, 1987
detailsDescription: Typed letter sent by the Ferrari Commercial Office (ref. CO/EDC/pf/346) to Mr. Edward L.C. Smith in Santa Cruz, California, dated September 24, 1987. The office informs the recipient that his previous letter of August 14 was forwarded to them by Enzo Ferrari. In response to his interest in the new F40 model, the office clarifies that a series of cars compliant with USA regulations will be produced and marketed through the official sales network in his country. The letter concludes by suggesting that Mr. Smith contact his nearest Ferrari dealer as soon as possible.    No further handwritten annotations are present on the document.

freeSearchText: ID: 11041  Path: Gene Stout, October 29, 1987 (11041)  Gene Stout October 29 1987 Typed letter sent by Mr. Gene Stout from Mentor Ohio to Enzo Ferrari dated October 29 1987. The author long-time admirer of the brand writes expressing his interest in the new F40 model after reading about it in American automotive magazines. He requests technical literature brochures and photographs of the F40 and other Ferrari models. The document features handwritten annotation in red ink 40 at top indicating subject for internal filing. No administrative signs of reply are present on sheet. detailsDescription: Letter written by a U.S. fan, Mr. Scott Simmons, who learned about the new F40 GT project.  He compliments the decision to return to producing racing cars, on which Ferrari's reputation was built, and has no doubts about the success of the operation.  He also hopes that in F1 the team will become stable enough to rise to the top ranks and finally asks for an autograph.   No administrative signs of a reply are present on the sheet.

He would like to obtain two cars of the model one for his frequent trips to Europe and other for his ranch in Montana. He hopes that being Ferrari owner 31 years company can grant him this wish.  then informs of problem that has happened friend of his with 412 which he asks support in solving. In Post Scriptum he confesses that was very happy have participated in Imola celebrations.  Enzo replies Mr. Lee interest two cars cannot overcome difficulties producing limited number. therefore suggests choose one two versions secure F40 time.  Regarding problem Mr. Godchaux III 412 explains immediately recommended inconvenience those charge.  September 21 1987 21-09-1987 21-09-1987 Robert M. https://f1947.archivio.com/SYSTEM - Archivio Storico Pubblico Ferrari (Ente Comunicazione)/THRON DIGITAL ARCHIVE/SPORTS CARS/1987_F40/00_/04_CORR/1987 CORR/CORR_T_1987_016.jpeg
https://f1947.archivio.com/SYSTEM - Archivio Storico Pubblico Ferrari (Ente Comunicazione)/THRON DIGITAL ARCHIVE/SPORTS CARS/1987_F40/00_/04_CORR/1987 CORR/CORR_T_1987_017.jpeg

detailsDescription: Typed letter from collector Robert M. Lee addressed to Enzo Ferrari dated September 21, 1987. He thanks Enzo Ferrari for the meeting he had with Della Casa and Tazzioli regarding the F40. He would like to obtain two cars of the model, one for his frequent trips to Europe and the other for his ranch in Montana. He hopes that, being a Ferrari owner for 31 years, the company can grant him this wish.  He then informs of a problem that has happened to a friend of his with a 412 for which he asks for support in solving. In the Post Scriptum he confesses that he was very happy to have participated in the Imola celebrations.     Enzo Ferrari replies that Mr. Lee's interest in the two cars cannot overcome the difficulties in producing a limited number. He therefore suggests that he choose one of the two versions to secure the F40 in time.  Regarding the problem of Mr. Godchaux III's 412, he explains that he immediately recommended the inconvenience to those in charge.

freeSearchText: ID: 11045  Path: Ferrari General Management, October 14, 1987 (11045)  Ferrari General Management October 14 1987 Typewritten letter from Ferrari General Management addressed to former British racing driver Sterling Moss regarding the request made to Enzo Ferrari about the F40.  Conceived as road car and not racing car the car will have an EE licence plate and driving it or bringing it back to England via on trailer is his responsibility.  Finally they inform that they will do everything accommodate him regarding his personalized request for chassis number.  October 14 1987 14-10-1987 14-10-1987 General Management Letter Other 680 Inglese Cartaceo Folder Corrispondenza Enzo 1987 Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Correspondence 5891 Private letters 5892 10953 October 14 11045

detailsDescription: Typewritten letter from Ferrari's General Management addressed to former British racing driver Sterling Moss regarding the request made to Enzo Ferrari about the F40.  Conceived as a road car and not a racing car, the car will have an EE licence plate and driving it or bringing it back to England via on trailer is his responsibility.   Finally, they inform that they will do everything to accommodate him regarding his personalized request for the chassis number.

detailsDescription: The book traces the history of Ferrari through a chronological overview, model by model, of the entire Ferrari production, from the Auto Avio Costruzioni 815 of 1940, the ‘Ferrari preview’, to the F 2004 single-seater. Each monographic sheet is completed by the technical data of the model, which reports the main characteristics relating to the engine, transmission, car body, chassis, dimensions, weight and performance.

detailsDescription: Xerxes DZB, 100 jaar, 1904-2004 is the official commemorative volume published for the centenary of the historic Dutch football club XerxesDZB in Rotterdam.
